lessons.title: Knight Fork Practice
lessons.description: Find the Knight fork that wins material!

steps.instruction: Your Knight on e5 is beautifully placed. Find the fork that attacks the Queen AND f7!

feedback.correct: Brilliant! Nxf7 forks the Queen on d8 and the Rook on h8! This is the 'Royal Fork'!
feedback.incorrect: Look at what Nxf7 would attack - the Queen on d8 and the Rook on h8!

lessons.successMessage: The Knight fork is one of the most powerful tactics!
hints: Look at what the Knight could attack from f7

lessons.title: Pin the Knight
lessons.description: Use a pin to win material.

steps.instruction: Black's Knight on f6 is protecting something important. Can you pin it?

feedback.correct: Excellent! The Knight is now pinned to the Queen. It can't move without losing the Queen!
feedback.incorrect: Try Bg5 - this pins the Knight to the Queen behind it.

lessons.successMessage: Pins are devastating! The piece can't move without bigger losses.
hints: A pin attacks a piece that can't move
Look for a Bishop move to g5

lessons.title: Back Rank Checkmate

steps.instruction: Black's King is trapped behind its pawns. Find the checkmate!

feedback.correct: CHECKMATE! The Rook delivers mate on the back rank. The pawns trap their own King!
feedback.incorrect: Move a Rook to the 8th rank - the King can't escape because of its own pawns!

lessons.successMessage: Back rank mates are beautiful! Always watch for them.
hints: The King can't escape through its own pawns
Put a Rook on the 8th rank

lessons.title: Discovered Attack
lessons.description: Use a discovered attack to win the Queen!

steps.instruction: Your Bishop on c4 is blocking your Queen's attack on f7. Move the Bishop to DISCOVER an attack!

feedback.correct: Brilliant! Bxf7+ is check, AND the Queen was already attacking f7. Now you win material!
feedback.incorrect: Try Bxf7+ - it's check AND it uncovers the Queen's attack on f7. Devastating!

lessons.successMessage: Discovered attacks are like getting two moves in one!
hints: The Queen wants to attack f7
Moving the Bishop will 'discover' the Queen's attack

lessons.title: Skewer the King
lessons.description: Use a skewer to win the Queen!

steps.instruction: The Black King and Queen are aligned on the a-file. Find the skewer!

feedback.correct: Skewer! The Rook attacks the King on a5. When the King moves, you capture the Queen on a8!
feedback.incorrect: Try Ra5+ - the Rook skewers the King and Queen along the a-file!

lessons.successMessage: Skewers are like reverse pins - the more valuable piece is in front and must move!
hints: A skewer attacks a piece, and when it moves, you capture what's behind
Attack the King along the a-file
